title: Baloghia montana
taxonomy: Baloghia montana is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant is native to New Caledonia, and its classification is further broken down into the subfamily Acalyphoideae. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 300 genera and 2,600 species. Baloghia montana is an endemic species found in the island's tropical rainforests. The classification of Baloghia montana is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plant species.

intro: Baloghia montana is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the Mountain Baloghia. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Baloghia montana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
